A BINDING-CONSTRAINT diagnostic: per density point, report the fraction of contacts where the byte budget actually bound (offered > transferred) and the buffer-full fraction. This distinguishes a true airtime/TTL-limited delivery curve from a mere contact-graph-percolation curve, and tells you whether the budget abstraction is doing any work.
An ENSEMBLE + uncertainty requirement: N seeds per density point, curves with confidence bands, cliff located with an uncertainty range — not a single deterministic line.

A SENSITIVITY MATRIX as a DONE criterion: the cliff location reported as a range across {mobility model: gathering vs RWP vs static-uniform}, {boundary: bounded vs torus}, {budget B: binding/marginal/non-binding}, {eviction policy}, and {Δt halved}. If the cliff is stable across these, trust it; if not, the README must report the spread. The current DONE ("the curve shows the cliff for a documented param set") is too weak — a single param set can show a fooled cliff.

EXPLICIT MODELING-ASSUMPTIONS / CAVEATS section in the README listing every idealization (unit-disk links, zero setup overhead unless added, no contention unless added, RWP optimism, abstracted reconciliation) and the DIRECTION each biases delivery (optimistic/pessimistic), so downstream readers never mistake the curve for measured BLE physics.
A Δt convergence test and the constraint max_speed*Δt << r documented in the config, so contact detection is not an artifact of step size.

A DELIVERY-RATIO DENOMINATOR definition (fair-chance cohort: only blobs with >= TTL of in-window time after creation) and a first-hold-only counting assertion test (deliver->evict->re-acquire counts once; latency = first hold), to prevent end-of-run cohort bias and duplicate/resurrection inflation.

A grounded PARAMETER PROVENANCE table: cite the source for assumed BLE throughput (~50 KB/s app-layer, 1M PHY + DLE, minus ATT/L2CAP overhead), setup cost t_setup, contact-duration distribution, and the gathering-scenario area/N — so B and density are traceable to reality, not invented.

mustFix.title: Define the message workload and the delivery-ratio denominator — without it the headline metric is undefined
mustFix.fix: Add a Workload sub-model to SCOPE IN and to the config dataclass. Specify: (1) message count M and generation process — default to a single fixed cohort injected at warm-up end (cleanest first curve) with sweepable rate as an option; (2) src/dst selection = uniform-random distinct pairs; (3) the exact ratio: delivery_ratio = (messages first-held by recipient within absolute TTL) / (messages in the fair-chance cohort). Define the fair-chance cohort as messages whose creation_ts leaves >= one max-TTL of in-window simulated time after creation (excludes right-censored end-of-run messages). Confirm in spec that the measured load is light enough not to itself saturate budget B (else you measure congestion, not percolation). Add a known-answer test pinning the denominator. Flagged independently by 4 of 5 lenses (scope-YAGNI rated it critical).

mustFix.title: Add a percolation-validation harness against ground truth before trusting any curve
mustFix.fix: Make the FIRST integration test a static, uniform-random (or Poisson) placement on a TORUS (periodic boundary) with B=inf, TTL=inf, buffer=inf. Assert its giant-component / delivery knee lands near the known continuum-percolation critical mean degree (~4.5) within a stated tolerance. This validates contact detection and the density axis against theory and gates everything downstream. Promote STATIC uniform placement to the PRIMARY headline-cliff configuration (it is the regime where N*pi*r^2/A literally equals the theory's reduced density); treat RWP as a mobility overlay measured against this baseline. Without this gate you cannot distinguish real physics from an engine bug. Raised by measurement-validity, design-fidelity, scope-YAGNI, and testability lenses.

mustFix.title: Fix the density axis: RWP border effect, speed decay, and boundary topology corrupt the one quantity being measured
mustFix.fix: Three required additions: (a) Boundary mode becomes an explicit logged config field — TORUS for the validation/cliff sweep (matches infinite-plane theory), walls for the realistic-venue scenario (report the expected upward threshold shift separately). (b) RWP stationarity: enforce v_min > 0 (documented) to kill speed decay, and either use perfect/steady-state RWP initialization (Le Boudec-Vojnovic) OR a documented warm-up window discarded from all metrics. (c) Because RWP center-concentrates nodes, do NOT report nominal density alone — also compute and plot delivery against the EMPIRICAL/realized mean neighbor degree measured over the steady-state window. Add a stationarity sanity check (first-half vs second-half contact rate / mean degree within tolerance) that must pass before a curve is accepted. All 5 lenses raised RWP non-stationarity; misleading-results rated the border-effect axis corruption as critical.

mustFix.title: Mandate replications + confidence intervals per density point — a single seed cannot locate a cliff
mustFix.fix: Determinism-by-seed is necessary but not sufficient: variance is MAXIMAL at the percolation threshold, so one seed per density point is indistinguishable from noise exactly where the answer matters. Require R independent replications per density point (start R=20-30, denser near the cliff), with sub-seeds derived deterministically from a master seed (numpy SeedSequence.spawn or seed_i = hash(master, density_idx, i)). Report mean delivery with a 95% CI (Wilson interval for the ratio — better than normal near 0/1) and latency as a distribution (median + IQR/percentiles, not mean). Use finer density spacing in the transition region. Restate DoD #4 from 'curve clearly shows the cliff' (un-falsifiable eyeballing) to 'mean delivery crosses hi->lo with non-overlapping CIs across the transition band, and CI half-width < X at the cliff.' Flagged by all 5 lenses; two rated it critical.

mustFix.title: Make the byte budget density-aware and grounded — a constant B is structurally optimistic and can hide or fake the cliff
mustFix.fix: The parent's airtime thesis is that effective throughput COLLAPSES with density (3 BLE advert channels, no hopping, collisions at scale). A density-independent B makes airtime cheapest exactly where the parent says it gets expensive, and since the x-axis IS density, a density-dependent error changes the cliff's SHAPE (real systems can show delivery rise then FALL — contention-limited — which constant B cannot reproduce). Required: (1) model effective throughput as throughput_ideal / (1 + alpha * n_local_contenders) where n_local_contenders = peers within r at contact time; sweep/sensitivity-test alpha. (2) Add a fixed per-contact setup/handshake cost t_setup subtracted before any payload — contacts shorter than t_setup transfer ZERO (kills the 'free delivery on a 1-second brush' bias that inflates the sparse end). (3) Add a reconciliation decode-failure probability p_fail yielding zero useful transfer. (4) Quantize transfer to whole ~1KB blobs (0.9 blobs delivers 0). (5) Ground throughput/t_setup/contact-duration in CITED BLE figures (parameter-provenance table). (6) Sweep B at >=3 levels (binding/marginal/non-binding) and report a binding-constraint diagnostic: fraction of contacts where budget actually bound — if ~0 across the sweep, you are measuring contact-graph percolation, not airtime-limited delivery, and the budget abstraction is decorative. State in README that constant-B is an UPPER BOUND on delivery. Raised by design-fidelity and misleading-results (both high).

mustFix.title: Pin time-step dt and per-episode budget accounting — dt is a hidden knob that fabricates or destroys contacts and erases the airtime constraint
mustFix.fix: Add a CFL-style constraint to config validation, asserted at startup: v_max * dt << r (e.g. v_max*dt <= r/4 to r/5). Add a dt-convergence check to the validation plan: halving dt must change delivery ratio and mean contact duration by < a tolerance. Better: compute contact entry/exit analytically per straight-line RWP leg (closest-approach to segment) so contacts and durations are dt-independent. Define contact as an explicit state machine (out->in = start, in->in = ongoing, in->out = end) and charge budget B per contact EPISODE (entry to exit) tracked across steps, NOT per step — otherwise a long co-location transfers unbounded bytes and the airtime constraint vanishes. Unit-test all three transitions, the per-episode cap, a fast tangential fly-through (geometry tuned so naive sampling would miss it), and dt-convergence. Log dt and a missed-contact estimate in the CSV. Raised by measurement-validity, design-fidelity, testability, and misleading-results.

mustFix.title: Pin the RNG contract — 'deterministic by seed' is unbuildable as stated across numpy + python random and breaks under parallel replication
mustFix.fix: numpy global state and python's random module are separate generators; module-global np.random.seed()/random.seed() makes runs non-isolated (any incidental call advances global state) and blocks the per-replication parallelism that finding 'replications + CIs' requires. Mandate: inject a single numpy.random.Generator (np.random.default_rng(seed)) threaded explicitly through model/engine/policies; prefer ONE generator for everything; NO module-global np.random.*/random.seed anywhere (add a grep/lint test). Derive per-replication and per-component substreams via SeedSequence.spawn so order-independence holds. Strengthen the determinism test to byte-compare the FULL output CSV across two same-seed runs and assert two cells run in swapped order produce identical output. Raised by scope-YAGNI and testability (both high).

mustFix.title: Specify exchange semantics precisely — symmetry, offer-selection-under-scarcity, budget direction, and deterministic pair-iteration order all move the measured number
mustFix.fix: Specify in the spec: (1) B consumed per contact-episode, not per step (see dt finding). (2) Offer-selection order under scarcity (when both nodes have more to offer than B allows) is an explicit, swappable, seeded policy — default uniform-random among missing — and is a measured variable, since it decides what propagates under scarcity and interacts with the cliff. (3) B is per-direction or shared, justified against BLE half-duplex reality. (4) Fixed deterministic pair-iteration order (e.g. sorted by node id) added to the determinism test. (5) Buffer accept contract returns Accepted|RejectedSeen|RejectedExpired with accept-then-evict-to-fit semantics so the eviction unit test is a pure function. Raised by measurement-validity, design-fidelity, scope-YAGNI, and testability.

shouldConsider: Protect invariants 2 (pure flooding) and 4 (sealed-sender) IN CODE even though their anonymity value is not measured here: keep recipient/sender as scoring-only ground-truth labels held in the metrics layer / sim oracle, never readable by model/engine/policies. Forwarding, eviction, and exchange must operate only on {id, created_at, ttl, size}. Add an assertion/test that no policy or exchange function references sender/recipient — otherwise the engine can silently use addressing and make the curve optimistic vs the real uniform soup.

Disambiguate the eviction rule (currently read two opposite ways). State explicitly: under cap, evict from the OLDEST-by-creation cohort with randomized tie-break (retain younger), NOT closest-to-TTL, per parent 9.5; add the per-neighbor buffer-share cap (part of the same anti-flood design, affects how junk-flood degrades honest delivery). Add an exact-victim unit test on a hand-built buffer plus a property test (victim always in youngest/oldest band, never closest-to-TTL) reconciled with the injected-RNG determinism test. Cross-link the test name to parent 9.5.

Pin the seen-record contract: window W >= maxTTL + margin (parent 6), FIFO-by-time aging (not fingerprint-by-saturation), documented capacity asserted in the junk-flood test to NOT cause forgetting within W. Add a flood-past-capacity anti-resurrection test asserting no expired/evicted id is ever re-accepted within W and that delivery count == unique-recipient-first-hold count. Record seen-record occupancy/evictions in the CSV.

Promote circulation/airtime to a CO-HEADLINE on the same density sweep (not a secondary 'proxy'): report transmissions-per-delivered-message (overhead ratio) and per-contact budget utilization vs density. For a pure-flooding system the cost curve is co-equal — delivery can be ~100% in a regime that is operationally dead from airtime saturation. Near-zero extra cost since the engine already counts transfers.

Run contrasting-regime sweeps cheaply (config rows, not new code): (a) a reference regime buffer=inf/TTL=inf/B=inf to isolate the pure topological cliff as the baseline; (b) at least two density sweeps at contrasting buffer/TTL so the report can separate a connectivity cliff from buffer-saturation / TTL-expiry artifacts; (c) one contrasting eviction policy (random or FIFO) to show the cliff location is robust (or flag if not). Buffer size and TTL are first-order drivers of delivery, not background settings.

Add an optional readability-window delivery predicate: delivered iff held AND (now - created_at) <= readable_window <= ttl (default = ttl for v1, but the hook must exist and be sweepable). The parent's delivery is 'can READ it', not 'possesses ciphertext'; late arrival is highest exactly near the cliff where the headline matters. At minimum, README states arrival==delivery here is an upper bound relative to the parent's time-ratcheted forward secrecy.

Consider adding hop-energy as a first-class pluggable blob field (born at B, decrement-on-reshare, drop at 0) — the parent's real expiry backstop in the no-trusted-clock regime, which caps re-shares and limits epidemic spread (a delivery-affecting mechanic). Default non-binding for v1, but EITHER require a B sensitivity sweep OR add an explicit justified deferral note that an energy-unbounded flood overstates delivery in sparse density.

Replace RWP-as-default-headline with a gathering/clustered/hotspot mobility model faithful to invariant 7 (dense-gathering, not metropolis); RWP overestimates delivery ~20%+ and models an open field, not a venue. Keep RWP only as an explicitly-labeled optimistic calibration bound.

Make the cliff definition quantitative so DoD #4 is falsifiable and can back a regression assertion: fit a logistic/sigmoid to (density, delivery), report the midpoint (or delivery=0.5 crossing) with a CI from the replications. DoD: validation sweep recovers the static-percolation mean-degree threshold within tolerance AND the dynamic sweep reports a midpoint estimate with CI.

Add a full parameter manifest into every CSV (W, H, N, r, speed dist + v_min, dt, B/throughput/t_setup, alpha, buffer cap, TTL, boundary mode, mobility mode, warm-up, horizon, M, lambda_msg, injection window, master seed, replication index) so any curve point is independently reproducible — a seed alone does not reproduce a result. Plus a README 'fidelity-to-parent' traceability table mapping each modeled mechanic to its parent section and listing each abstraction with the DIRECTION of its bias (this slice's curve is an UPPER BOUND on delivery).

Present latency JOINTLY with delivery ratio at every density and annotate it as conditional-on-delivery (survivorship bias: hardest messages drop out near the cliff, so mean latency can paradoxically improve as the system worsens). Consider a censoring-robust fixed-deadline metric (fraction delivered within deadline Delta) which is more decision-relevant for a TTL-bounded system.

Specify the contact-radius inequality (recommend dist <= r, compare squared distances dist2 <= r*r to avoid sqrt and keep boundary tests version-stable), and specify an O(N) spatial index (uniform grid / cell-list bucketed by r) with a brute-force-equivalence test on small N plus a perf smoke test — the 50k-node sweep x R replications is infeasible with O(N^2) all-pairs and slowness will erode the replication count.

Add a configurable background junk/load fraction and report the curve at >=2 junk levels (0% and realistic non-zero) so the clean curve is not mistaken for expected real-world performance, and the byte-budget abstraction is stress-tested under airtime contention.

Add an explicit MODELING-ASSUMPTIONS / CAVEATS section to the README listing every idealization (unit-disk links, setup/contention only if added, RWP optimism, abstracted reconciliation, arrival==delivery) with the direction each biases delivery, so downstream readers never mistake the curve for measured BLE physics.
synthesis.scopeCheck: The first-slice boundary is CORRECT and well-judged — arguably the strongest part of the spec. All five lenses independently endorsed 'locate the delivery-vs-density percolation cliff before any phone code' as the right P0 move, faithfully exercising the delivery-governing invariants (1 offline, 2 pure flooding, 5 absolute TTL, 7 gathering-scale) while correctly deferring the orthogonal ones (3 anonymity-uniformity, 4 sealed-sender, 6 no-server-identity) plus crypto/tokens/PoSW/real-PHY/bridges. The byte-budget-instead-of-IBLT abstraction is a defensible YAGNI call for THIS question. Two scope adjustments are needed, neither of which is a major re-cut: (1) the slice is slightly UNDER-scoped on the controls that determine whether the cliff is even a percolation phenomenon — it must add a static/torus percolation-validation baseline, replications+CIs, a workload model, and contrasting buffer/TTL/B regimes (all cheap config rows on the same engine, not new architecture); and (2) airtime/circulation should be promoted from secondary proxy to co-headline, since for a pure-flooding system the cost curve is co-equal with the delivery curve. Caveat that must be enforced in-code even though out-of-scope for measurement: invariants 2 and 4 require that forwarding/eviction/exchange never read sender/recipient. Do NOT expand into crypto/anonymity/mobile/full multi-factor sweeps — those remain correctly deferred.","
synthesis.signoffRecommendation: CONDITIONAL APPROVAL — do not start the build on the current spec, but the concept and slice boundary are sound and this is a fix-the-spec exercise, not a re-think. All five independent lenses returned 'sound-with-fixes' (none said needs-rework), and their high/critical findings converge on the same short list of root causes, which is a strong signal. The risk is specific and well-understood: as written, the sim would produce a confident, reproducible, and WRONG delivery-vs-density curve that the team would then trust — the density axis is corrupted by RWP non-uniformity, the cliff is unverifiable from single-seed runs, the byte budget is optimistically density-independent, dt is an unpinned confound, and the headline metric's denominator is undefined. Mandate the eight MUST-FIX items as spec revisions before any code: (1) workload + delivery-ratio-denominator definition, (2) a static/torus percolation-validation harness that must recover the ~4.5 mean-degree threshold as the gating first test, (3) density-axis fixes (boundary topology + RWP stationarity + empirical-degree reporting), (4) replications with 95% CIs replacing eyeballed DoD, (5) density-aware grounded byte budget with a binding-constraint diagnostic, (6) pinned dt with per-episode budget accounting, (7) an explicit injected-Generator RNG contract, (8) precise exchange semantics. Re-review the revised spec (a quick second pass, not a full fan-out) to confirm the MUST-FIX items landed, then green-light the build. Of the three sign-off decisions: stack=Python is fine (numpy + cell-list index; keep the Rust hot-path option only if profiling demands it); the first-slice boundary is approved with the two scope adjustments above; mobility default should change — make STATIC the primary cliff probe and RWP a labeled optimistic overlay rather than the headline default.
